title: fix: repoint gptimage to new Azure resource (eastus2 blocked)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Azure blocked image generation on `myceli-prod-eastus2` (resource-level content-policy block — every `gptimage`/`gptimage-large` request returned 403, surfaced to users as a misleading 422 "content_policy_violation")
- Verified live: even a benign "red car" prompt 403s; chat on the same resources still works, so the block is image-modality-specific
- Repoint `gptimage` (gpt-image-1-mini) and `gptimage-large` (gpt-image-1.5) to a fresh `myceli-prod-img-westus3` resource — both deployments verified returning 200
- Add `AZURE_MYCELI_PROD_IMG_WESTUS3_API_KEY` to sops secrets (prod/staging/dev) + worker bindings; CI pushes it on deploy

Fixes #12067

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Moving the studio out West
gist.blurb: Our GPT image models hit a roadblock, so we packed up and moved them to a fresh new server out West. The pixels are flowing smoothly once again!
gist.summary: Repointed the gptimage and gptimage-large models to a new Azure westus3 resource to resolve 403 content policy errors from the previous eastus2 endpoint.
gist.impact: Users will no longer receive false-positive 422 content policy violations for benign image prompts when using the GPT image models.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot plugging a glowing cable into a new server rack, while the robot companion holds up a freshly generated picture of a red car (referencing the benign prompt from the PR description).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot plugging a glowing lime green (#ecf874) cable into a new retro server rack. Nearby, a chunky pixel robot with a monitor head happily holds up a freshly generated pixel painting of a red car. Soft pastel gradient background with warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
